mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 7.5 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery phase indicate manufacturing bottlenecks that constrain output growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines during recovery periods, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline market trends in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Lower core inflation readings reduce real interest rate pressures and preserve corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day returns show no directional bias following CPI declines.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent quarter-over-quarter signals a demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of low-growth GDP observations, or the threshold effect vanishes when adjusting for lagged market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price levels above 230 during a late-cycle expansion trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when CPI exceeds 230 in late-cycle regimes, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction after controlling for yield curve slope.
